mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 40% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real consumer credit and housing starts indicate acute demand destruction and balance sheet stress, triggering sharp negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no significant drawdown in SPX or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive baselines in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding factory output and rising capacity utilization, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for three consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of recovery-phase observations or the correlation with 20-day returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase surpassing 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger months or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for nominal yield curve steepening.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills that support corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03 or payroll growth periods show no statistically significant outperformance versus baseline market returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly real GDP growth and monthly real consumer credit signals unsustainable consumption financed by debt, leading to negative equity returns over the next month as credit cycles reverse.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds GDPC1 growth by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: The divergence metric fails to predict negative returns or shows positive predictive power in walk-forward tests over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and housing starts during a late-cycle expansion indicates weakening aggregate demand and inventory buildup, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered observations or the joint signal loses predictive significance when tested against a univariate baseline.
